topic: The Metallurgical Crucible: Wartime Material Experiments and the Evolution of Modern Alloys
thesis: Modern conflict repeatedly turned material limits into engineering deadlines, and the alloys born under wartime pressure became the foundation of modern aerospace, medicine, nuclear engineering, and industrial life.
audience: curious documentary viewers who like military history, engineering, and science storytelling
pacing: five-act documentary with strong cold open, measured technical explanation, and high-intensity transitions between alloy stories
constraints: Use rights-cleared archival footage and museum/open-access imagery first.
Use generated video only for microscopic alloy animations and abstract transitions after approval.
Avoid operational weapon-making instructions; focus on history, material science, and industrial consequences.
Final render blocked until real media assets and rights records are attached.
